title: Agent-driven local Categorizer launch for Cursor browser UAT probes

AC-1.description: dev-environment.json declares start_command, health_url, poll_seconds, env_file, required_env_vars, browser_probe_url
AC-1.notes: T-0041 creates the real .cursor/dev-environment.json with all AC-1 fields (start_command, health_url, poll_seconds:60, poll_interval_seconds:2, env_file, required_env_vars, browser_probe_url, connect.health_path:/). T-0042 creates .env.example matching env_file contract.

AC-2.description: Docker Compose service config supports ephemeral local launch without breaking production
AC-2.notes: T-0040 creates docker-compose.local.yml per DEC-0018: standalone file with explicit -f flag, service firefly-ai-categorizer, port 3001:3000, no Traefik labels, restart:no. No override/auto-merge risk with parent stack.

AC-3.description: Local service URL reachable from Cursor IDE browser MCP — via direct port binding with explicit notes in runbook
AC-3.notes: T-0040 provides direct port binding (3001:3000). T-0043 implements the launch script that makes the URL reachable. T-0044 documents port binding in runbook. T-0045 documents the probe workflow.

AC-4.description: Agent can trigger launch, wait for health, open browser_probe_url
AC-4.notes: T-0043 creates scripts/dev-launch.sh (launch + health poll 60s/2s). T-0045 documents the agent probe workflow. T-0047 is the E2E test verifying the full flow.

AC-5.description: Browser UAT probe collects console error evidence and /api/reviews response without JSON SyntaxError on HTML
AC-5.notes: T-0045 documents the probe workflow including console error collection via CDP and /api/reviews response handling (JSON success vs HTML structured error). T-0047 is E2E test including /api/reviews JSON check.

AC-6.notes: T-0046 creates user guide with standard schema (Purpose, Prerequisites, Usage, Example, Limitations, Troubleshooting) and USER_GUIDE_MODE=1.

T-0042.notes: Fixes latent docs bug (R-0023 Q3): runbook references .env.example but file was never committed.

T-0044.description: Runbook section for local agent-driven launch
T-0044.notes: Documents direct port binding, ephemeral nature, cross-refs to user guide + architecture.

gap_analysis.summary: No gaps found. All 6 acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-6) have at least one associated task.

orphan_analysis.summary: No orphan tasks. All 8 tasks reference at least one acceptance criterion from US-0006.

details.notes: No hard deps in JSON. Implicit runtime dependency on T-0040 (compose file) — acceptable; script references docker-compose.local.yml which is created in parallel Phase 1.
Dependencies valid — needs compose (T-0040) and dev-env JSON (T-0041) to document them.

Dependency valid — needs launch script (T-0043) to document/validate the probe workflow. Note: tasks.md summary says 'depends on T-0040' but JSON says T-0043. JSON is authoritative; both Phase 1 so no conflict.
Dependencies valid — needs compose (T-0040), dev-env JSON (T-0041), and launch script (T-0043) to document the full workflow.
Dependency valid. Note: tasks.md summary lists T-0043,T-0044,T-0045 as deps; JSON is identical. All referenced tasks exist and are earlier in the chain.

tasks_md_vs_json_discrepancies.resolution: JSON is authoritative. T-0043 is the more precise dependency (launch script). tasks.md overview table may have listed T-0040 but JSON spec says T-0043. Both are Phase 1 so no scheduling impact.

findings.title: T-0043 implicit dependency on T-0040 (compose file)
findings.description: T-0043 (dev-launch.sh) has no explicit dependency on T-0040 (docker-compose.local.yml) in its JSON. The script invokes 'docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml' — it requires the compose file to exist at runtime. Both are Phase 1 parallel tasks, so this is acceptable: the execution order guarantees T-0040 completes before T-0043 is attempted (since they are in the same parallel phase, both must succeed before Phase 2). Not a blocker; could be improved by adding T-0040 to T-0043's depends_on for explicitness.

findings.title: tasks.md vs JSON dependency discrepancy for T-0045
findings.description: tasks.md overview table lists T-0045 dependencies as 'T-0043' (correct) but tasks.md body says 'depends on T-0043' (also correct). Earlier sprint-plan summary listed 'T-0040'. The JSON (T-0043) is authoritative. No impact — tasks.md body is consistent with JSON.

findings.title: AC-1 health_url targets GET / not GET /health
findings.description: AC-1 text mentions 'health_url reachable at GET http://localhost:<port>/health' but the architectural decision (R-0023, DEC-0018) resolved the health probe target as GET / (matching Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK and App.js root route). T-0041 correctly implements health_url: http://localhost:3001/ and connect.health_path: /. AC-1 is covered by the resolution of this discrepancy in earlier phases. The AC text predates the research phase resolution; the task correctly follows the resolved design.

findings.title: T-0045 broad AC coverage (AC-3, AC-4, AC-5)
findings.description: T-0045 references AC-3, AC-4, and AC-5 in its JSON. This is acceptable since it documents the integrated probe workflow that spans those concerns. However, the task description says it primarily 'documents' the workflow — actual validation is deferred to T-0047. Ensure T-0045 deliverable includes both documentation AND at least a dry-run validation attempt (even if Docker is unavailable, the attempt should be documented).

findings.title: T-0047 E2E test requires Docker daemon — conditional execution
findings.description: T-0047 E2E test has a conditional execution path: 'If Docker unavailable locally, document as not-executed with rationale.' This is noted in the task. During QA phase, verify that T-0047 was either fully executed OR explicitly documented as not-executed with Docker unavailable rationale. Partial execution (health poll only, no browser probe) would leave AC-5 unverified.

summary.recommendation: Proceed to execute. All 6 acceptance criteria have task coverage. No critical gaps. Minor findings are non-blocking and can be addressed during execution/QA phases.
